title: ICT Macros Every Time and Every Hour! (xx:50 to xx:10)
author: macr0trader
selftext:
Same model every time! I literally just copy past...  
Try yourself!

If you only use macros you are already in the right path! This is not a joke...

This made me start making money in prop firms, i have more than 30k in payouts just by using this.

ICT Macros are the most powerful thing you can use, its based on **TIME**

ICT always say \*\*"TIME AND PRICE"\*\*and this is the truth.

Macros are a time window of 20minutes, every hour is a macro, from xx:50 to xx:10

***Example***:

01:50 to 02:10

04:50 to 05:10

09:50 to 10:10

20:50 to 21:10

Etc...

**What is a macro?**

\-A macro is a function in the alghoritm. When the macro starts, price will do a expansion or a reversal.

\-Its our job to figure out what the macro will do.

**What time is the macro?**

\-Every hour is a macro.

\-A macro is a time window beetween xx:50 to xx:10

***Example:***

01:50 to 02:10

04:50 to 05:10

09:50 to 10:10

20:50 to 21:10

Etc...

**Why the macro is important?**

\-ICT told us "Price and Time", this is the secret most people dont understand!

\-When time is not correct, price will not move!

\-You dont need to watch the chart for 5...6 hours a day. you just need to show up at the right moment!

\-BEFORE ANY EXPANSION MOVE, the macro will be in place, EVERY!

**Remember:**

TIME AND PRICE

comments.author: Cylosmagic
comments.body: Time is more important then price 👩🏾‍🎨

comments.author: Shot_Loan_354
comments.body: so.. you must be a billionaire now that you know this secret.

comments.author: Kaszrak
comments.body:
This entire thing collapses the moment you apply even basic logic instead of Reddit mysticism.

Let’s start with the obvious. A Telegram link, a “school,” a YouTube channel, and the classic “I will never ask for money, don’t fall for scammers” disclaimer. That sentence exists for one reason only. Funnel building. Anyone who has spent five minutes around real trading knows nobody with a real edge leads with moral reassurance. That’s a retail confidence trick, not professional behavior.

Now to the substance, or lack of it.

There is no such thing as a universal algorithm that runs every hour on a fixed twenty minute schedule. That idea is laughable to anyone who has sat on a bank floor. Markets are not governed by a single system. They are fragmented across venues, asset classes, participants, jurisdictions, and objectives. FX spot, futures, equities, rates, options all trade for different reasons, on different clocks, with different liquidity dynamics. Pretending they all obey the same hourly ritual is fantasy.

The “macro” definition is especially embarrassing. Expansion or reversal. That literally covers every possible outcome except price not moving, which conveniently gets blamed on the trader. If price goes up, macro worked. If it goes down, macro worked. If it chops, you “misread it.” That is not a model. That is an unfalsifiable belief system. On a real desk, anything that cannot be invalidated gets laughed out of the room because it cannot be risk managed.

The xx:50 to xx:10 window is not precision. It’s padding. Forty minutes is an eternity intraday. Of course something eventually happens somewhere in that window. That’s not prediction, that’s waiting long enough for randomness to hand you a screenshot.

The algorithm talk is pure cosplay. Execution algos respond to liquidity, volatility, spread, inventory, and urgency. They do not synchronize on the hour like monks ringing a bell. There is no “macro function” that switches on because the minute hand moved. That language only exists to impress people who have never seen an order book at scale.

Time does matter, but not in this cartoon way. Time matters because of known structural flows. Session opens. Fixings. Data releases. Option expiries. Rebalancing. Those are tied to real obligations and real size. Not “every hour forever, trust me bro.”

The prop firm payout flex proves nothing. Evaluation rules reward tight stops, time based participation, and small size. Weak models survive there all the time. Put real capital behind this, with real drawdown limits and market impact, and it dies fast. That’s why this lives on Reddit and Telegram instead of in actual portfolios.

Calling this “the truth” is the final tell. Markets do not have truths. They have mechanisms and probabilities. Anyone selling a single hidden truth that explains everything is not teaching trading. They are selling certainty to people who don’t know how markets work yet.

So no, this is not institutional knowledge. It’s retail astrology with a clock instead of planets, wrapped in ICT branding, distributed through Telegram, and protected by a model that can never be wrong.

If this were real, he wouldn’t need a school, a channel, or a disclaimer. He’d be quietly scaling size until liquidity stopped him. And liquidity does not care what time his macro says it is. 🤦‍♂️

replies.author: Alternative-Load8809
replies.body: Best comment I have ever read on the Internet. Exceptionally well said, sir 🤌

comments.author: Twisting_Juniper
comments.body: I genuinely have no idea how anybody can fall for this guy's bullshit.
